description: Upper right paratracheal station above the azygos arch region in the IASLC map.
accessNotes: Usually reviewed with a superior right paratracheal sweep and careful vessel orientation.
memoryCues: Pair it mentally with 4R as a vertical stack.

description: Upper left paratracheal station near the superior mediastinal contour.
accessNotes: Use left-sided vascular relationships and laterality cues to separate it from 4L.
memoryCues: High left paratracheal, above the 4L stack.
Use laterality first, then border detail.

description: Lower right paratracheal station extending toward the right main bronchus.
accessNotes: A core EBUS staging target that should remain easy to spot in map and explorer modules.
memoryCues: Remember it as the anchor beneath 2R.

description: Lower left paratracheal station that pairs with 2L in the superior-to-inferior stack.
accessNotes: Learners benefit from a clear contrast between 4L and adjacent left hilar stations.
memoryCues: Anchor it with the aortic-side mental map.

description: Subcarinal station beneath the carina and one of the most frequently reviewed core staging stations.
accessNotes: Use it as a central landmark when orienting the map and explorer modules.
memoryCues: Treat it as the bridge between right and left hilar pathways.

description: Right hilar station adjacent to the right main bronchus entry zone.
accessNotes: Best taught relative to 4R and 11R so learners can recognize the handoff from mediastinal to hilar landmarks.
memoryCues: Think main bronchus before lobar branching.

description: Left hilar station paired with 4L and 11L in the left-sided pathway.
accessNotes: Learners often need repeated reinforcement of the distinction between 4L and 10L.
memoryCues: Use it as the bridge from paratracheal to lobar landmarks.

description: Right interlobar station extending beyond the right hilar entry landmark.
accessNotes: Teach it as a downstream branch from 10R to reduce confusion.
memoryCues: Right interlobar after the hilar gate.
Use branching as the mental clue.

description: Left interlobar station paired with 10L in the left hilar pathway.
accessNotes: Use the explorer checklist to reinforce its branching relationship from the left hilar station.
memoryCues: Left interlobar after the hilar checkpoint.
Think branch-level cue, not carinal cue.
